cards.name: Perk Hunter
cards.description: Worth a billion and still in it for the catering.

cards.name: Icky Touch
cards.description: Why women don't want to be in the boardroom.

cards.name: Uncle Pete
cards.description: Who needs expertise when you have a private jet?

cards.name: Rubber Stamper
cards.description: Approves everything. Questions nothing. Perfect attendance.

cards.name: CEO's Pet
cards.description: What conflict of interest?

cards.name: Board Observer
cards.description: Nevermind me—I just want to get the know the business.

cards.name: Ancient Emeritus
cards.description: Email will change everything.

cards.name: Equity Hostage
cards.description: Can't contribute. Can attend meetings though.

cards.name: Serial Entrepreneur
cards.description: Has an exit, a podcast, and an opinion on everything.

cards.name: Captain Hat Guy
cards.description: Does owning a bunch of yachts make you a Captain?

cards.name: In Absentia
cards.description: Sends regrets and a strongly worded email after every decision.

cards.name: The General, Ret.
cards.description: Business is like a battlefield... I mean an army... or a tank?

cards.name: Former Secretary of Something
cards.description: Even their bathroom has mahogany walls.

cards.name: Cult Cultivator CEO
cards.description: Mandatory meditation at 6am. Voluntary employment.

cards.name: Acqui-holic CEO
cards.description: Integration is someone else's problem.

cards.name: Move Fast, Break People CEO
cards.description: The bodies are metaphorical. The lawsuits are not.

cards.name: The Outsourcer CMO
cards.description: Let's use India for that.

cards.name: Attribution Thief CMO
cards.description: "Marketing optimizations" trump product innovations every time.

cards.name: Backlog Bomber CPO
cards.description: I added a few tickets—should only take a few sprints.

cards.name: Funnel Fetishist CPO
cards.description: Every human interaction is a conversion opportunity.

cards.name: Tech Debt Theologian CTO
cards.description: Can explain exactly why everything is broken. Has no plan to fix it.

cards.name: CTO, IC
cards.description: Doesn't trust software they didn't write themselves.

cards.name: Process Preacher COO
cards.description: If it isn't in Confluence, it didn't happen.

cards.name: Vanishing Act COO
cards.description: Killer onboarding energy. Completely checked out by month four.

cards.name: Burn Warden CFO
cards.description: Sends daily emails about "discipline" while expensing $400 meals.

cards.name: Token Adult CFO
cards.description: No idea what the product is, but the investors feel better.

cards.name: STEM Program Intern
cards.description: Built their first app at 14. Still can't work the coffee machine.

cards.name: Investor's Kid
cards.description: Someone needs to prompt Claude Code.

cards.name: Friday Night Shipper
cards.description: What could go wrong?

cards.name: Junior Engineer
cards.description: Will mass-refactor your codebase on day one.

cards.name: Over-Architect
cards.description: Writes enterprise-grade infrastructure for a product with 12 users.

cards.name: The Sandbagger
cards.description: 13 points (two days).

cards.name: Rabbit Hole Delver
cards.description: Doesn't everyone take three days for a 1-point ticket?

cards.name: Whiz Kid
cards.description: If they're so smart, why are they here?

cards.name: The Quiet Contrarian
cards.description: Says nothing in meetings, then builds something completely different.

cards.name: Hindsight Expert
cards.description: I knew this would happen.

cards.name: Scrum Watchdog
cards.description: How do we know if people are standing when their cameras are off?

cards.name: Preservationist
cards.description: We can't change that—nobody knows how it works.

cards.name: Platform Gatekeeper
cards.description: Owns the deploy pipeline. Answers Slack on their own schedule.

cards.name: The Scope Slayer
cards.description: What if we just... didn't build that?

cards.name: CTO's Pet
cards.description: Too busy to work with the team.

cards.name: Energy Drink Addict
cards.description: Commits code at 3am. Reverts it at 9am.

cards.name: QA Tyrant
cards.description: Filed 200 bugs. Twelve of them matter.

cards.name: Grizzled Vet
cards.description: Has survived four reorgs and two pivots.

cards.name: 10x Engineer
cards.description: Produces ten times the code and ten times the opinions.

cards.name: Agentic Overlord
cards.description: Replaced half the team with a YAML file.

cards.name: Mom & Dad
cards.description: They believe in you. They don't understand what you do.

cards.name: Golf Buddy
cards.description: Met the founder at a charity scramble. Wrote a check on the ninth hole.

cards.name: Former Child Actor
cards.description: Looking for a second act that doesn't involve a stage.

cards.name: Trust Fund Kid
cards.description: The Patagonia vest makes him an authority.

cards.name: Spray and Prayer
cards.description: Portfolio of 47 startups. Can name five of them.

cards.name: Pivot Pusher
cards.description: Funded your idea, now has a better one for you.

cards.name: Trend Chaser
cards.description: Only invests in whatever category was on the cover of TechCrunch last month.

cards.name: Car Dealership King
cards.description: Cash rich, tech illiterate, very confident.

cards.name: FOMO Financier
cards.description: Only invested because another firm showed interest.

cards.name: Gas Station Tycoon
cards.description: Thinks SaaS is a type of fuel additive.

cards.name: Offshore Opportunist
cards.description: Checks come from countries we've never heard of.

cards.name: Real Estate Mogul
cards.description: Wants to know when the company will buy its own building.

cards.name: Retired Quarterback
cards.description: Great arm. Questionable understanding of unit economics.

cards.name: Ketamine Cowboy
cards.description: Microdoses on board call days.

cards.name: Secret Saudi
cards.description: The wire transfer came from a holding company in the Caymans.

cards.name: Your Cousin's Kid
cards.description: Her portfolio is a mood board.

cards.name: Building Maintenance Guy
cards.description: Got roped into fixing the website because he fixed the WiFi once.

cards.name: High School Mentee
cards.description: Here for community service hours. Accidentally useful.

cards.name: Design Camp Grad
cards.description: I know the Figma.

cards.name: Design Intern
cards.description: Has heard of users.

cards.name: Scope Creeper
cards.description: Just one more thing before we ship.

cards.name: D3
cards.description: The double-dipping designer.

cards.name: Remote Ghost
cards.description: Sorry, just went for a walk.

cards.name: 10x Meeting Time PM
cards.description: Blocks your calendar to talk about the next meeting.

cards.name: Pixel Tweaker
cards.description: That icon is a little off and everyone needs to know.

cards.name: Project Manager
cards.description: Don't we already have a PM?

cards.name: Tech Hipster Designer
cards.description: Stood in line for the latest Apple Pencil.

cards.name: Infinite Discovery
cards.description: 47 interviews and still not ready to share insights.

cards.name: Certified Product Owner
cards.description: You know, Claude can write tickets too.

cards.name: Scrumerfall PM
cards.description: Agile in the streets, waterfall in the sheets.

cards.name: Vision Designer
cards.description: Designs for the product three pivots from now.

cards.name: Deck Artisan
cards.description: The pitch deck has more polish than the product.

cards.name: Product Mentor
cards.description: Hasn't shipped since 2011 but has very strong opinions.

cards.name: Design Guru
cards.description: Their Dribbble has 40k followers. Their last shipped feature has 12 users.

cards.name: Buzzword Dispenser
cards.description: Let's leverage our synergies to ideate a north star metric.

cards.name: Empty Desk
cards.description: Nobody remembers hiring them. Nobody's asked them to leave.

cards.name: Neighbor's Kid
cards.description: Crushing it on TikTok, apparently.

cards.name: Who is this kid?
cards.description: He just came in and sat down.

cards.name: Cousin Drew
cards.description: Family discount. Family performance.

cards.name: BD Spammer
cards.description: 400 LinkedIn messages a day. Two replies.

cards.name: Crypto Bro
cards.description: Wants to put the customer database on the blockchain.

cards.name: Headhunter
cards.description: Finds talent. Bills you for the search.

cards.name: A.B.C.
cards.description: Always Be Calling product managers to demand new features.

cards.name: Hustler
cards.description: The deal will close, but you don't want to know how.

cards.name: Self-Imagined Hotshot
cards.description: He's a legend... according to him.

cards.name: Suspiciously Energetic
cards.description: Why does he keep going out to his car?

cards.name: Whale Whisperer
cards.description: Knows three Fortune 500 CTOs by first name.

cards.name: High Roller
cards.description: Only takes meetings at steakhouses.

cards.name: Office Cleanse
cards.description: Can burning sage prevent burning runway?

cards.name: Keg in the Kitchen
cards.description: Of course you can drink it. But, you know, we'll know.

cards.name: Dusty Ping Pong Table
cards.description: Last used at the holiday party before the pandemic.

cards.name: Networking event (all dudes)
cards.description: Great turnout. Zero diversity.

cards.name: Catered Lunch
cards.description: Let's TACO 'bout it hahaha.

cards.name: "Unlimited" PTO
cards.description: You could take off next week, hypothetically.

cards.name: All Hands Meeting
cards.description: Could have been an email. Wasn't.

cards.name: Standing Desks
cards.description: Most aren't even plugged in.

cards.name: Slack Premium
cards.description: Because you need all 3 years of arguments on record.

cards.name: Swag Drop
cards.description: Three hundred waterbottles that break if you stare too hard.

cards.name: Team Offsite
cards.description: Two days of trust falls, rope climbs, and oblivious ableism.

cards.name: Nap Pod
cards.description: HR says it's for wellness. Engineers say it's for deploys.

cards.name: Recruiting Firm
cards.description: 25% of a year's salary to lightly filter resumes.

cards.name: Podcast Sponsorship
cards.description: Now brought to you by a company you'll forget in ten seconds.

cards.name: AWS Contract
cards.description: Burns cash faster than your engineers.

cards.name: Executive Coach
cards.description: Charges $500/hr to tell the CEO what everyone else says for free.

cards.name: Salesforce Integration
cards.description: Two months to implement a dashboard nobody checks.

cards.name: Branding Agency
cards.description: Spent $200k to change the blue.

cards.name: Unicorn Dreamer
cards.description: Drives an Uber on the weekends and hopes to retire at 35.

cards.name: Not a Numbers Guy
cards.description: Negotiated their 0.5% equity to 0.25%.

cards.name: RevOps Evader
cards.description: You can't measure what isn't reported.

cards.name: Whale Chaser
cards.description: If we could just land that deal with Costco...

cards.name: The Wolf of Our Street
cards.description: Learned everything they know from 'Boiler Room'.

cards.name: Quota Balancer
cards.description: Never misses a target, but never goes over it either.

cards.name: The Vibe Closer
cards.description: No deck, no plan, just a feeling.
